/* set \"correct\" referer when following\n                             location: */\n  BIT(opt_no_body);    /* as set with CURLOPT_NOBODY */\n  BIT(verbose);        /* output verbosity */\n  BIT(krb);            /* Kerberos connection requested */\n  BIT(reuse_forbid);   /* forbidden to be reused, close after use */\n  BIT(reuse_fresh);    /* do not reuse an existing connection  */\n  BIT(no_signal);      /* do not use any signal/alarm handler */\n  BIT(tcp_nodelay);    /* whether to enable TCP_NODELAY or not */\n  BIT(ignorecl);       /* ignore content length */\n  BIT(http_te_skip);   /* pass the raw body data to the user, even when\n                          transfer-encoded (chunked, compressed) */\n  BIT(http_ce_skip);   /* pass the raw body data to the user, even when\n                          content-encoded (chunked, compressed) */\n  BIT(proxy_transfer_mode); /* set transfer mode (;type=<a|i>) when doing\n                               FTP via an HTTP proxy */\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  BIT(socks5_gssapi_nec); /* Flag to support NEC SOCKS5 server */\n#endif\n  BIT(sasl_ir);         /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\n  BIT(tcp_keepalive);  /* use TCP keepalives */\n  BIT(tcp_fastopen);   /* use TCP Fast Open */\n  BIT(ssl_enable_alpn);/* TLS ALPN extension? */\n  BIT(path_as_is);     /* allow dotdots? */\n  BIT(pipewait);       /* wait for multiplex status before starting a new\n                          connection */\n  BIT(suppress_connect_headers); /* suppress proxy CONNECT response headers\n                                    from user callbacks */\n  BIT(dns_shuffle_addresses); /* whether to shuffle addresses before use */\n  BIT(haproxyprotocol); /* whether to send HAProxy PROXY protocol v1\n                           header */\n  BIT(abstract_unix_socket);\n  BIT(disallow_username_in_url); /* disallow username in url */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  BIT(doh); /* DNS-over-HTTPS enabled */\n  BIT(doh_verifypeer);     /* DoH certificate peer verification */\n  BIT(doh_verifyhost);     /* DoH certificate hostname verification */\n  BIT(doh_verifystatus);   /* DoH certificate status verification */\n#endif\n  BIT(http09_allowed); /* allow HTTP/0.9 responses */\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n  BIT(ws_raw_mode);\n#endif\n};\n\nstruct Names {\n  struct Curl_hash *hostcache;\n  enum {\n    HCACHE_NONE,    /* not pointing to anything */\n    HCACHE_MULTI,   /* points to a shared one in the multi handle */\n    HCACHE_SHARED   /* points to a shared one in a shared object */\n  } hostcachetype;\n};\n\n/*\n * The 'connectdata' struct MUST have all the connection oriented stuff as we\n * may have several simultaneous connections and connection structs in memory.\n *\n * The 'struct UserDefined' must only contain data that is set once to go for\n * many (perhaps) independent connections. Values that are generated or\n * calculated internally for the \"session handle\" must be defined within the\n * 'struct UrlState' instead.\n */\n\nstruct Curl_easy {\n  /* First a simple identifier to easier detect if a user mix up this easy\n     handle with a multi handle. Set this to CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER */\n  unsigned int magic;\n  /* once an easy handle is tied to a connection cache\n     a non-negative number to distinguish this transfer from\n     other using the same cache. For easier tracking\n     in log output.\n     This may wrap around after LONG_MAX to 0 again, so it\n     has no uniqueness guarantee for very large processings. */\n  curl_off_t id;\n\n  /* first, two fields for the linked list of these */\n  struct Curl_easy *next;\n  struct Curl_easy *prev;\n\n  struct connectdata *conn;\n  struct Curl_llist_element connect_queue; /* for the pending and msgsent\n                                              lists */\n  struct Curl_llist_element conn_queue; /* list per connectdata */\n\n  CURLMstate mstate;  /* the handle's state */\n  CURLcode result;   /* previous result */\n\n  struct Curl_message msg; /* A single posted message. */\n\n  /* Array with the plain socket numbers this handle takes care of, in no\n     particular order. Note that all sockets are added to the sockhash, where\n     the state etc are also kept. This array is mostly used to detect when a\n     socket is to be removed from the hash. See singlesocket(). */\n  curl_socket_t sockets[MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE];\n  unsigned char actions[MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE]; /* action for each socket in\n                                                    sockets[] */\n  int numsocks;\n\n  struct Names dns;\n  struct Curl_multi *multi;    /* if non-NULL, points to the multi handle\n                                  struct to which this \"belongs\" when used by\n                                  the multi interface */\n  struct Curl_multi *multi_easy; /* if non-NULL, points to the multi handle\n                                    struct to which this \"belongs\" when used\n                                    by the easy interface */\n  struct Curl_share *share;    /* Share, handles global variable mutexing */\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n  struct PslCache *psl;        /* The associated PSL cache. */\n#endif\n  struct SingleRequest req;    /* Request-specific data */\n  struct UserDefined set;      /* values set by the libcurl user */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  struct CookieInfo *cookies;  /* the cookies, read from files and servers.\n                                  NOTE that the 'cookie' field in the\n                                  UserDefined struct defines if the \"engine\"\n                                  is to be used or not. */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  struct hsts *hsts;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  struct altsvcinfo *asi;      /* the alt-svc cache */\n#endif\n  struct Progress progress;    /* for all the progress meter data */\n  struct UrlState state;       /* struct for fields used for state info and\n                                  other dynamic purposes */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  struct WildcardData *wildcard; /* wildcard download state info */\n#endif\n  struct PureInfo info;        /* stats, reports and info data */\n  struct curl_tlssessioninfo tsi; /* Information about the TLS session, only\n                                     valid after a client has asked for it */\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\n  struct hyptransfer hyp;\n#endif\n\n  /* internal: true if this easy handle was created for internal use and the\n     user does not have ownership of the handle. */\n  bool internal;\n};\n\n#define LIBCURL_NAME \"libcurl\"\n\n#endif /* HEADER_CURL_URLDATA_H */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " /**",
        "  * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others",
        "  * on the same connection.",
        "- * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218",
        "+ * Supports both HTTP/2 (weight+dependencies) and HTTP/3 (RFC 9218 urgency+incremental)",
        "  */",
        " struct Curl_data_priority {",
        " #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 1239,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 1239
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        " #ifdef USE_NGHTTP2",
        "   BIT(exclusive);",
        " #endif",
        "+#if defined(USE_HTTP3)",
        "+  /* HTTP/3 RFC 9218 Extensible Priorities */",
        "+  unsigned int urgency;  /* 0-7, default 3 */",
        "+  BIT(incremental);      /* incremental flag */",
        "+#endif",
        " };",
        " ",
        " /*"
      ],
      "newLines": 11,
      "newStart": 1251,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 1251
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false

    "content": "  int tcp_keepidle;     /* seconds in idle before sending keepalive probe */\n  int tcp_keepintvl;    /* seconds between TCP keepalive probes */\n\n  size_t maxconnects;    /* Max idle connections in the connection cache */\n\n  long expect_100_timeout; /* in milliseconds */\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  struct Curl_data_priority priority;\n#endif\n  curl_resolver_start_callback resolver_start; /* optional callback called\n                                                  before resolver start */\n  void *resolver_start_client; /* pointer to pass to resolver start callback */\n  long upkeep_interval_ms;      /* Time between calls for connection upkeep. */\n  multidone_func fmultidone;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  struct Curl_easy *dohfor; /* this is a DoH request for that transfer */\n#endif\n  CURLU *uh; /* URL handle for the current parsed URL */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  void *trailer_data; /* pointer to pass to trailer data callback */\n  curl_trailer_callback trailer_callback; /* trailing data callback */\n#endif\n  char keep_post;     /* keep POSTs as POSTs after a 30x request; each\n                         bit represents a request, from 301 to 303 */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/urldata.h",

57. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",
  "new_string": "#endif\n  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {\n#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n    const char *h3priority = va_arg(param, const char *);\n    if(h3priority) {\n      /* Parse h3-priority string: u=N[,i]\n       * u=0-7 (urgency), optional ,i (incremental) */\n      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default */\n      int incremental = 0;\n\n      if(sscanf(h3priority, \"u=%u\", &urgency) == 1) {\n        /* Validate urgency is in range 0-7 */\n        if(urgency > 7)\n          urgency = 7;\n        data->set.priority.urgency = urgency;\n\n        /* Check for incremental flag */\n        if(strchr(h3priority, 'i')) {\n          data->set.priority.incremental = TRUE;\n        } else {\n          data->set.priority.incremental = FALSE;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;\n    break;",
  "old_string": "#endif\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;\n    break;",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",
  "newString": "#endif\n  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {\n#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n    const char *h3priority = va_arg(param, const char *);\n    if(h3priority) {\n      /* Parse h3-priority string: u=N[,i]\n       * u=0-7 (urgency), optional ,i (incremental) */\n      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default */\n      int incremental = 0;\n\n      if(sscanf(h3priority, \"u=%u\", &urgency) == 1) {\n        /* Validate urgency is in range 0-7 */\n        if(urgency > 7)\n          urgency = 7;\n        data->set.priority.urgency = urgency;\n\n        /* Check for incremental flag */\n        if(strchr(h3priority, 'i')) {\n          data->set.priority.incremental = TRUE;\n        } else {\n          data->set.priority.incremental = FALSE;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;\n    break;",

  "originalFile": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n#include <netinet/in.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_LINUX_TCP_H\n#include <linux/tcp.h>\n#elif defined(HAVE_NETINET_TCP_H)\n#include <netinet/tcp.h>\n#endif\n\n#include \"urldata.h\"\n#include \"url.h\"\n#include \"progress.h\"\n#include \"content_encoding.h\"\n#include \"strcase.h\"\n#include \"share.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n#include \"warnless.h\"\n#include \"sendf.h\"\n#include \"http2.h\"\n#include \"setopt.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"\n#include \"altsvc.h\"\n#include \"hsts.h\"\n\n/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\n#include \"curl_printf.h\"\n#include \"curl_memory.h\"\n#include \"memdebug.h\"\n\nCURLcode Curl_setstropt(char **charp, const char *s)\n{\n  /* Release the previous storage at `charp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n     copy of `s'. Return CURLE_OK or CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*charp);\n\n  if(s) {\n    if(strlen(s) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    *charp = strdup(s);\n    if(!*charp)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nCURLcode Curl_setblobopt(struct curl_blob **blobp,\n                         const struct curl_blob *blob)\n{\n  /* free the previous storage at `blobp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n     copy of blob. If CURL_BLOB_COPY is set, the data is copied. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*blobp);\n\n  if(blob) {\n    struct curl_blob *nblob;\n    if(blob->len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    nblob = (struct curl_blob *)\n      malloc(sizeof(struct curl_blob) +\n             ((blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) ? blob->len : 0));\n    if(!nblob)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    *nblob = *blob;\n    if(blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) {\n      /* put the data after the blob struct in memory */\n      nblob->data = (char *)nblob + sizeof(struct curl_blob);\n      memcpy(nblob->data, blob->data, blob->len);\n    }\n\n    *blobp = nblob;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setstropt_userpwd(char *option, char **userp, char **passwdp)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  char *user = NULL;\n  char *passwd = NULL;\n\n  /* Parse the login details if specified. It not then we treat NULL as a hint\n     to clear the existing data */\n  if(option) {\n    size_t len = strlen(option);\n    if(len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    result = Curl_parse_login_details(option, len,\n                                      (userp ? &user : NULL),\n                                      (passwdp ? &passwd : NULL),\n                                      NULL);\n  }\n\n  if(!result) {\n    /* Store the username part of option if required */\n    if(userp) {\n      if(!user && option && option[0] == ':') {\n        /* Allocate an empty string instead of returning NULL as user name */\n        user = strdup(\"\");\n        if(!user)\n          result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n\n      Curl_safefree(*userp);\n      *userp = user;\n    }\n\n    /* Store the password part of option if required */\n    if(passwdp) {\n      Curl_safefree(*passwdp);\n      *passwdp = passwd;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n#define C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(x) (x & 0xffff)\n#define C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(x) (x & 0xffff0000)\n\nstatic CURLcode protocol2num(const char *str, curl_prot_t *val)\n{\n  if(!str)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  if(curl_strequal(str, \"all\")) {\n    *val = ~(curl_prot_t) 0;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  *val = 0;\n\n  do {\n    const char *token = str;\n    size_t tlen;\n\n    str = strchr(str, ',');\n    tlen = str? (size_t) (str - token): strlen(token);\n    if(tlen) {\n      const struct Curl_handler *h = Curl_builtin_scheme(token, tlen);\n\n      if(!h)\n        return CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL;\n\n      *val |= h->protocol;\n    }\n  } while(str && str++);\n\n  if(!*val)\n    /* no protocol listed */\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * Do not make Curl_vsetopt() static: it is called from\n * packages/OS400/ccsidcurl.c.\n */\nCURLcode Curl_vsetopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option, va_list param)\n{\n  char *argptr;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  long arg;\n  unsigned long uarg;\n  curl_off_t bigsize;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n\n    data->set.dns_cache_timeout = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CA_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n\n    data->set.general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_USE_GLOBAL_CACHE:\n    /* deprecated */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection for proxy */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES)) {\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES)) {\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites for proxy */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_RANDOM_FILE:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_EGDSOCKET:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXCONNECTS:\n    /*\n     * Set the absolute number of maximum simultaneous alive connection that\n     * libcurl is allowed to have.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxconnects = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FORBID_REUSE:\n    /*\n     * When this transfer is done, it must not be left to be reused by a\n     * subsequent transfer but shall be closed immediately.\n     */\n    data->set.reuse_forbid = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FRESH_CONNECT:\n    /*\n     * This transfer shall not use a previously cached connection but\n     * should be made with a fresh new connect!\n     */\n    data->set.reuse_fresh = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_VERBOSE:\n    /*\n     * Verbose means infof() calls that give a lot of information about\n     * the connection and transfer procedures as well as internal choices.\n     */\n    data->set.verbose = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set to include the header in the general data output stream.\n     */\n    data->set.include_header = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS:\n    /*\n     * Shut off the internal supported progress meter\n     */\n    data->set.hide_progress = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    if(data->set.hide_progress)\n      data->progress.flags |= PGRS_HIDE;\n    else\n      data->progress.flags &= ~PGRS_HIDE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOBODY:\n    /*\n     * Do not include the body part in the output data stream.\n     */\n    data->set.opt_no_body = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    if(data->set.opt_no_body)\n      /* in HTTP lingo, no body means using the HEAD request... */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_HEAD;\n    else if(data->set.method == HTTPREQ_HEAD)\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FAILONERROR:\n    /*\n     * Don't output the >=400 error code HTML-page, but instead only\n     * return error.\n     */\n    data->set.http_fail_on_error = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_KEEP_SENDING_ON_ERROR:\n    data->set.http_keep_sending_on_error = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD:\n  case CURLOPT_PUT:\n    /*\n     * We want to sent data to the remote host. If this is HTTP, that equals\n     * using the PUT request.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg) {\n      /* If this is HTTP, PUT is what's needed to \"upload\" */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_PUT;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      /* In HTTP, the opposite of upload is GET (unless NOBODY is true as\n         then this can be changed to HEAD later on) */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_REQUEST_TARGET:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TARGET],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FILETIME:\n    /*\n     * Try to get the file time of the remote document. The time will\n     * later (possibly) become available using curl_easy_getinfo().\n     */\n    data->set.get_filetime = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SERVER_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT:\n    /*\n     * Option that specifies how quickly a server response must be obtained\n     * before it is considered failure. For pingpong protocols.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 0) && (arg <= (INT_MAX/1000)))\n      data->set.server_response_timeout = (unsigned int)arg * 1000;\n    else\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_NO_OPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Option that prevents libcurl from sending TFTP option requests to the\n     * server.\n     */\n    data->set.tftp_no_options = va_arg(param, long) != 0;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_BLKSIZE:\n    /*\n     * TFTP option that specifies the block size to use for data transmission.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.tftp_blksize = arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC:\n    /*\n     * Parse the $HOME/.netrc file\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_NETRC_IGNORED) || (arg >= CURL_NETRC_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.use_netrc = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC_FILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.netrc file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_NETRC_FILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFERTEXT:\n    /*\n     * This option was previously named 'FTPASCII'. Renamed to work with\n     * more protocols than merely FTP.\n     *\n     * Transfer using ASCII (instead of BINARY).\n     */\n    data->set.prefer_ascii = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION:\n    /*\n     * Set HTTP time condition. This must be one of the defines in the\n     * curl/curl.h header file.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_TIMECOND_NONE) || (arg >= CURL_TIMECOND_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.timecondition = (unsigned char)(curl_TimeCond)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE:\n    /*\n     * This is the value to compare with the remote document with the\n     * method set with CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION\n     */\n    data->set.timevalue = (time_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * This is the value to compare with the remote document with the\n     * method set with CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION\n     */\n    data->set.timevalue = (time_t)va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSLVERSION:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLVERSION:\n#endif\n    /*\n     * Set explicit SSL version to try to connect with, as some SSL\n     * implementations are lame.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    {\n      long version, version_max;\n      struct ssl_primary_config *primary = &data->set.ssl.primary;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n      if(option != CURLOPT_SSLVERSION)\n        primary = &data->set.proxy_ssl.primary;\n#endif\n\n      arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n      version = C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(arg);\n      version_max = C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(arg);\n\n      if(version < CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT ||\n         version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv2 ||\n         version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv3 ||\n         version >= CURL_SSLVERSION_LAST ||\n         version_max < CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_NONE ||\n         version_max >= CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_LAST)\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n      primary->version = (unsigned char)version;\n      primary->version_max = (unsigned int)version_max;\n    }\n#else\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n\n    /* MQTT \"borrows\" some of the HTTP options */\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MQTT)\n  case CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS:\n    /*\n     * A string with POST data. Makes curl HTTP POST. Even if it is NULL.\n     * If needed, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE must have been set prior to\n     *  CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS and not altered later.\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n\n    if(!argptr || data->set.postfieldsize == -1)\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], argptr);\n    else {\n      /*\n       *  Check that requested length does not overflow the size_t type.\n       */\n\n      if((data->set.postfieldsize < 0) ||\n         ((sizeof(curl_off_t) != sizeof(size_t)) &&\n          (data->set.postfieldsize > (curl_off_t)((size_t)-1))))\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else {\n        char *p;\n\n        (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n\n        /* Allocate even when size == 0. This satisfies the need of possible\n           later address compare to detect the COPYPOSTFIELDS mode, and\n           to mark that postfields is used rather than read function or\n           form data.\n        */\n        p = malloc((size_t)(data->set.postfieldsize?\n                            data->set.postfieldsize:1));\n\n        if(!p)\n          result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        else {\n          if(data->set.postfieldsize)\n            memcpy(p, argptr, (size_t)data->set.postfieldsize);\n\n          data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS] = p;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfields = data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS];\n    data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS:\n    /*\n     * Like above, but use static data instead of copying it.\n     */\n    data->set.postfields = va_arg(param, void *);\n    /* Release old copied data. */\n    (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n    data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The size of the POSTFIELD data to prevent libcurl to do strlen() to\n     * figure it out. Enables binary posts.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    if(data->set.postfieldsize < bigsize &&\n       data->set.postfields == data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      /* Previous CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS is no longer valid. */\n      (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n      data->set.postfields = NULL;\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfieldsize = bigsize;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * The size of the POSTFIELD data to prevent libcurl to do strlen() to\n     * figure it out. Enables binary posts.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    if(data->set.postfieldsize < bigsize &&\n       data->set.postfields == data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      /* Previous CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS is no longer valid. */\n      (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n      data->set.postfields = NULL;\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfieldsize = bigsize;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_AUTOREFERER:\n    /*\n     * Switch on automatic referer that gets set if curl follows locations.\n     */\n    data->set.http_auto_referer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING:\n    /*\n     * String to use at the value of Accept-Encoding header.\n     *\n     * If the encoding is set to \"\" we use an Accept-Encoding header that\n     * encompasses all the encodings we support.\n     * If the encoding is set to NULL we don't send an Accept-Encoding header\n     * and ignore an received Content-Encoding header.\n     *\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && !*argptr) {\n      argptr = Curl_all_content_encodings();\n      if(!argptr)\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else {\n        result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ENCODING], argptr);\n        free(argptr);\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ENCODING], argptr);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFER_ENCODING:\n    data->set.http_transfer_encoding = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION:\n    /*\n     * Follow Location: header hints on an HTTP-server.\n     */\n    data->set.http_follow_location = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_UNRESTRICTED_AUTH:\n    /*\n     * Send authentication (user+password) when following locations, even when\n     * hostname changed.\n     */\n    data->set.allow_auth_to_other_hosts =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS:\n    /*\n     * The maximum amount of hops you allow curl to follow Location:\n     * headers. This should mostly be used to detect never-ending loops.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxredirs = arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTREDIR:\n    /*\n     * Set the behavior of POST when redirecting\n     * CURL_REDIR_GET_ALL - POST is changed to GET after 301 and 302\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_301 - POST is kept as POST after 301\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_302 - POST is kept as POST after 302\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_303 - POST is kept as POST after 303\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_ALL - POST is kept as POST after 301, 302 and 303\n     * other - POST is kept as POST after 301 and 302\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < CURL_REDIR_GET_ALL)\n      /* no return error on too high numbers since the bitmask could be\n         extended in a future */\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.keep_post = arg & CURL_REDIR_POST_ALL;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POST:\n    /* Does this option serve a purpose anymore? Yes it does, when\n       CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS isn't used and the POST data is read off the\n       callback! */\n    if(va_arg(param, long)) {\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPPOST:\n    /*\n     * Set to make us do HTTP POST. Legacy API-style.\n     */\n    data->set.httppost = va_arg(param, struct curl_httppost *);\n    data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST_FORM;\n    data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_AWS)\n  case CURLOPT_AWS_SIGV4:\n    /*\n     * String that is merged to some authentication\n     * parameters are used by the algorithm.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_AWS_SIGV4],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    /*\n     * Basic been set by default it need to be unset here\n     */\n    if(data->set.str[STRING_AWS_SIGV4])\n      data->set.httpauth = CURLAUTH_AWS_SIGV4;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_REFERER:\n    /*\n     * String to set in the HTTP Referer: field.\n     */\n    if(data->state.referer_alloc) {\n      Curl_safefree(data->state.referer);\n      data->state.referer_alloc = FALSE;\n    }\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SET_REFERER],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->state.referer = data->set.str[STRING_SET_REFERER];\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERAGENT:\n    /*\n     * String to use in the HTTP User-Agent field\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_USERAGENT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set a list with proxy headers to use (or replace internals with)\n     *\n     * Since CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER was the only way to set HTTP headers for a\n     * long time we remain doing it this way until CURLOPT_PROXYHEADER is\n     * used. As soon as this option has been used, if set to anything but\n     * NULL, custom headers for proxies are only picked from this list.\n     *\n     * Set this option to NULL to restore the previous behavior.\n     */\n    data->set.proxyheaders = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_HEADEROPT:\n    /*\n     * Set header option.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    data->set.sep_headers = (bool)((arg & CURLHEADER_SEPARATE)? TRUE: FALSE);\n    break;\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIE:\n    /*\n     * Cookie string to send to the remote server in the request.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COOKIE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIEFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set cookie file to read and parse. Can be used multiple times.\n     */\n    argptr = (char *)va_arg(param, void *);\n    if(argptr) {\n      struct curl_slist *cl;\n      /* general protection against mistakes and abuse */\n      if(strlen(argptr) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      /* append the cookie file name to the list of file names, and deal with\n         them later */\n      cl = curl_slist_append(data->set.cookielist, argptr);\n      if(!cl) {\n        curl_slist_free_all(data->set.cookielist);\n        data->set.cookielist = NULL;\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n      data->set.cookielist = cl; /* store the list for later use */\n    }\n    else {\n      /* clear the list of cookie files */\n      curl_slist_free_all(data->set.cookielist);\n      data->set.cookielist = NULL;\n\n      if(!data->share || !data->share->cookies) {\n        /* throw away all existing cookies if this isn't a shared cookie\n           container */\n        Curl_cookie_clearall(data->cookies);\n        Curl_cookie_cleanup(data->cookies);\n      }\n      /* disable the cookie engine */\n      data->cookies = NULL;\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIEJAR:\n    /*\n     * Set cookie file name to dump all cookies to when we're done.\n     */\n  {\n    struct CookieInfo *newcookies;\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COOKIEJAR],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n\n    /*\n     * Activate the cookie parser. This may or may not already\n     * have been made.\n     */\n    newcookies = Curl_cookie_init(data, NULL, data->cookies,\n                                  data->set.cookiesession);\n    if(!newcookies)\n      result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    data->cookies = newcookies;\n  }\n  break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIESESSION:\n    /*\n     * Set this option to TRUE to start a new \"cookie session\". It will\n     * prevent the forthcoming read-cookies-from-file actions to accept\n     * cookies that are marked as being session cookies, as they belong to a\n     * previous session.\n     *\n     * In the original Netscape cookie spec, \"session cookies\" are cookies\n     * with no expire date set. RFC2109 describes the same action if no\n     * 'Max-Age' is set and RFC2965 includes the RFC2109 description and adds\n     * a 'Discard' action that can enforce the discard even for cookies that\n     * have a Max-Age.\n     *\n     * We run mostly with the original cookie spec, as hardly anyone implements\n     * anything else.\n     */\n    data->set.cookiesession = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_COOKIELIST:\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n\n    if(!argptr)\n      break;\n\n    if(strcasecompare(argptr, \"ALL\")) {\n      /* clear all cookies */\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n      Curl_cookie_clearall(data->cookies);\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n    }\n    else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \"SESS\")) {\n      /* clear session cookies */\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n      Curl_cookie_clearsess(data->cookies);\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n    }\n    else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \"FLUSH\")) {\n      /* flush cookies to file, takes care of the locking */\n      Curl_flush_cookies(data, FALSE);\n    }\n    else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \"RELOAD\")) {\n      /* reload cookies from file */\n      Curl_cookie_loadfiles(data);\n      break;\n    }\n    else {\n      if(!data->cookies)\n        /* if cookie engine was not running, activate it */\n        data->cookies = Curl_cookie_init(data, NULL, NULL, TRUE);\n\n      /* general protection against mistakes and abuse */\n      if(strlen(argptr) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      argptr = strdup(argptr);\n      if(!argptr || !data->cookies) {\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        free(argptr);\n      }\n      else {\n        Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n        if(checkprefix(\"Set-Cookie:\", argptr))\n          /* HTTP Header format line */\n          Curl_cookie_add(data, data->cookies, TRUE, FALSE, argptr + 11, NULL,\n                          NULL, TRUE);\n\n        else\n          /* Netscape format line */\n          Curl_cookie_add(data, data->cookies, FALSE, FALSE, argptr, NULL,\n                          NULL, TRUE);\n\n        Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\n        free(argptr);\n      }\n    }\n\n    break;\n#endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES */\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPGET:\n    /*\n     * Set to force us do HTTP GET\n     */\n    if(va_arg(param, long)) {\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION:\n    /*\n     * This sets a requested HTTP version to be used. The value is one of\n     * the listed enums in curl/curl.h.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    switch(arg) {\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE:\n#ifdef USE_HTTP2\n      /* TODO: this seems an undesirable quirk to force a behaviour on\n       * lower implementations that they should recognize independently? */\n      arg = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS;\n#endif\n      /* accepted */\n      break;\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_0:\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1:\n      /* accepted */\n      break;\n#ifdef USE_HTTP2\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_0:\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS:\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2_PRIOR_KNOWLEDGE:\n      /* accepted */\n      break;\n#endif\n#ifdef ENABLE_QUIC\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3:\n    case CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3ONLY:\n      /* accepted */\n      break;\n#endif\n    default:\n      /* not accepted */\n      if(arg < CURL_HTTP_VERSION_NONE)\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      return CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL;\n    }\n    data->set.httpwant = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_EXPECT_100_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    /*\n     * Time to wait for a response to an HTTP request containing an\n     * Expect: 100-continue header before sending the data anyway.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.expect_100_timeout = arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP09_ALLOWED:\n    arg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(arg > 1L)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\n    /* Hyper does not support HTTP/0.9 */\n    if(arg)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n#else\n    data->set.http09_allowed = arg ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#endif\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP200ALIASES:\n    /*\n     * Set a list of aliases for HTTP 200 in response header\n     */\n    data->set.http200aliases = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_SMTP) ||       \\\n    !defined(CURL_DISABLE_IMAP)\n# if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MIME)\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set a list with HTTP headers to use (or replace internals with)\n     */\n    data->set.headers = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n# endif\n\n# ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MIME\n  case CURLOPT_MIMEPOST:\n    /*\n     * Set to make us do MIME POST\n     */\n    result = Curl_mime_set_subparts(&data->set.mimepost,\n                                    va_arg(param, curl_mime *), FALSE);\n    if(!result) {\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST_MIME;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n      Curl_mime_cleanpart(data->state.formp);\n      Curl_safefree(data->state.formp);\n#endif\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MIME_OPTIONS:\n    data->set.mime_options = (unsigned int)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n# endif\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH:\n    /*\n     * Set HTTP Authentication type BITMASK.\n     */\n  {\n    int bitcheck;\n    bool authbits;\n    unsigned long auth = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n\n    if(auth == CURLAUTH_NONE) {\n      data->set.httpauth = auth;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    /* the DIGEST_IE bit is only used to set a special marker, for all the\n       rest we need to handle it as normal DIGEST */\n    data->state.authhost.iestyle =\n      (bool)((auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n\n    if(auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE) {\n      auth |= CURLAUTH_DIGEST; /* set standard digest bit */\n      auth &= ~CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE; /* unset ie digest bit */\n    }\n\n    /* switch off bits we can't support */\n#ifndef USE_NTLM\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM;    /* no NTLM support */\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM_WB; /* no NTLM_WB support */\n#elif !defined(NTLM_WB_ENABLED)\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM_WB; /* no NTLM_WB support */\n#endif\n#ifndef USE_SPNEGO\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NEGOTIATE; /* no Negotiate (SPNEGO) auth without\n                                    GSS-API or SSPI */\n#endif\n\n    /* check if any auth bit lower than CURLAUTH_ONLY is still set */\n    bitcheck = 0;\n    authbits = FALSE;\n    while(bitcheck < 31) {\n      if(auth & (1UL << bitcheck++)) {\n        authbits = TRUE;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n    if(!authbits)\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* no supported types left! */\n\n    data->set.httpauth = auth;\n  }\n  break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST:\n    /*\n     * Set a custom string to use as request\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CUSTOMREQUEST],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n\n    /* we don't set\n       data->set.method = HTTPREQ_CUSTOM;\n       here, we continue as if we were using the already set type\n       and this just changes the actual request keyword */\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPPROXYTUNNEL:\n    /*\n     * Tunnel operations through the proxy instead of normal proxy use\n     */\n    data->set.tunnel_thru_httpproxy = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYPORT:\n    /*\n     * Explicitly set HTTP proxy port number.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.proxyport = (unsigned short)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH:\n    /*\n     * Set HTTP Authentication type BITMASK.\n     */\n  {\n    int bitcheck;\n    bool authbits;\n    unsigned long auth = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n\n    if(auth == CURLAUTH_NONE) {\n      data->set.proxyauth = auth;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    /* the DIGEST_IE bit is only used to set a special marker, for all the\n       rest we need to handle it as normal DIGEST */\n    data->state.authproxy.iestyle =\n      (bool)((auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n\n    if(auth & CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE) {\n      auth |= CURLAUTH_DIGEST; /* set standard digest bit */\n      auth &= ~CURLAUTH_DIGEST_IE; /* unset ie digest bit */\n    }\n    /* switch off bits we can't support */\n#ifndef USE_NTLM\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM;    /* no NTLM support */\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM_WB; /* no NTLM_WB support */\n#elif !defined(NTLM_WB_ENABLED)\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NTLM_WB; /* no NTLM_WB support */\n#endif\n#ifndef USE_SPNEGO\n    auth &= ~CURLAUTH_NEGOTIATE; /* no Negotiate (SPNEGO) auth without\n                                    GSS-API or SSPI */\n#endif\n\n    /* check if any auth bit lower than CURLAUTH_ONLY is still set */\n    bitcheck = 0;\n    authbits = FALSE;\n    while(bitcheck < 31) {\n      if(auth & (1UL << bitcheck++)) {\n        authbits = TRUE;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n    if(!authbits)\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* no supported types left! */\n\n    data->set.proxyauth = auth;\n  }\n  break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy server:port to use as proxy.\n     *\n     * If the proxy is set to \"\" (and CURLOPT_SOCKS_PROXY is set to \"\" or NULL)\n     * we explicitly say that we don't want to use a proxy\n     * (even though there might be environment variables saying so).\n     *\n     * Setting it to NULL, means no proxy but allows the environment variables\n     * to decide for us (if CURLOPT_SOCKS_PROXY setting it to NULL).\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PRE_PROXY:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy server:port to use as SOCKS proxy.\n     *\n     * If the proxy is set to \"\" or NULL we explicitly say that we don't want\n     * to use the socks proxy.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PRE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy type.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLPROXY_HTTP) || (arg > CURLPROXY_SOCKS5_HOSTNAME))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.proxytype = (unsigned char)(curl_proxytype)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TRANSFER_MODE:\n    /*\n     * set transfer mode (;type=<a|i>) when doing FTP via an HTTP proxy\n     */\n    switch(va_arg(param, long)) {\n    case 0:\n      data->set.proxy_transfer_mode = FALSE;\n      break;\n    case 1:\n      data->set.proxy_transfer_mode = TRUE;\n      break;\n    default:\n      /* reserve other values for future use */\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      break;\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_AUTH:\n    data->set.socks5auth = (unsigned char)va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(data->set.socks5auth & ~(CURLAUTH_BASIC | CURLAUTH_GSSAPI))\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_PROXY */\n\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_GSSAPI_NEC:\n    /*\n     * Set flag for NEC SOCK5 support\n     */\n    data->set.socks5_gssapi_nec = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKS5_GSSAPI_SERVICE:\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SERVICE_NAME:\n    /*\n     * Set proxy authentication service name for Kerberos 5 and SPNEGO\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PROXY_SERVICE_NAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SERVICE_NAME:\n    /*\n     * Set authentication service name for DIGEST-MD5, Kerberos 5 and SPNEGO\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SERVICE_NAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_HEADERDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom pointer to pass the header write callback function\n     */\n    data->set.writeheader = (void *)va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ERRORBUFFER:\n    /*\n     * Error buffer provided by the caller to get the human readable\n     * error string in.\n     */\n    data->set.errorbuffer = va_arg(param, char *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_WRITEDATA:\n    /*\n     * FILE pointer to write to. Or possibly\n     * used as argument to the write callback.\n     */\n    data->set.out = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n#ifdef CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL\n  case CURLOPT_DIRLISTONLY:\n    /*\n     * An option that changes the command to one that asks for a list only, no\n     * file info details. Used for FTP, POP3 and SFTP.\n     */\n    data->set.list_only = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_APPEND:\n    /*\n     * We want to upload and append to an existing file. Used for FTP and\n     * SFTP.\n     */\n    data->set.remote_append = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_FILEMETHOD:\n    /*\n     * How do access files over FTP.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLFTPMETHOD_DEFAULT) || (arg >= CURLFTPMETHOD_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.ftp_filemethod = (unsigned char)(curl_ftpfile)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FTPPORT:\n    /*\n     * Use FTP PORT, this also specifies which IP address to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_FTPPORT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->set.ftp_use_port = (data->set.str[STRING_FTPPORT]) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT:\n    data->set.ftp_use_eprt = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV:\n    data->set.ftp_use_epsv = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_USE_PRET:\n    data->set.ftp_use_pret = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_SSL_CCC:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLFTPSSL_CCC_NONE) || (arg >= CURLFTPSSL_CCC_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.ftp_ccc = (unsigned char)(curl_ftpccc)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_SKIP_PASV_IP:\n    /*\n     * Enable or disable FTP_SKIP_PASV_IP, which will disable/enable the\n     * bypass of the IP address in PASV responses.\n     */\n    data->set.ftp_skip_ip = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_ACCOUNT:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_FTP_ACCOUNT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_ALTERNATIVE_TO_USER:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_FTP_ALTERNATIVE_TO_USER],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FTPSSLAUTH:\n    /*\n     * Set a specific auth for FTP-SSL transfers.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLFTPAUTH_DEFAULT) || (arg >= CURLFTPAUTH_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.ftpsslauth = (unsigned char)(curl_ftpauth)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_KRBLEVEL:\n    /*\n     * A string that defines the kerberos security level.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KRB_LEVEL],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->set.krb = (data->set.str[STRING_KRB_LEVEL]) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  case CURLOPT_FTP_CREATE_MISSING_DIRS:\n    /*\n     * An FTP/SFTP option that modifies an upload to create missing\n     * directories on the server.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    /* reserve other values for future use */\n    if((arg < CURLFTP_CREATE_DIR_NONE) ||\n       (arg > CURLFTP_CREATE_DIR_RETRY))\n      result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else\n      data->set.ftp_create_missing_dirs = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTQUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use after a transfer\n     */\n    data->set.postquote = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PREQUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use prior to RETR (Wesley Laxton)\n     */\n    data->set.prequote = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_QUOTE:\n    /*\n     * List of RAW FTP commands to use before a transfer\n     */\n    data->set.quote = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_READDATA:\n    /*\n     * FILE pointer to read the file to be uploaded from. Or possibly\n     * used as argument to the read callback.\n     */\n    data->set.in_set = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INFILESIZE:\n    /*\n     * If known, this should inform curl about the file size of the\n     * to-be-uploaded file.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.filesize = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INFILESIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * If known, this should inform curl about the file size of the\n     * to-be-uploaded file.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.filesize = bigsize;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT:\n    /*\n     * The low speed limit that if transfers are below this for\n     * CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_TIME, the transfer is aborted.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.low_speed_limit = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAX_SEND_SPEED_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * When transfer uploads are faster then CURLOPT_MAX_SEND_SPEED_LARGE\n     * bytes per second the transfer is throttled..\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_send_speed = bigsize;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAX_RECV_SPEED_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * When receiving data faster than CURLOPT_MAX_RECV_SPEED_LARGE bytes per\n     * second the transfer is throttled..\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_recv_speed = bigsize;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_TIME:\n    /*\n     * The low speed time that if transfers are below the set\n     * CURLOPT_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT during this time, the transfer is aborted.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.low_speed_time = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CURLU:\n    /*\n     * pass CURLU to set URL\n     */\n    data->set.uh = va_arg(param, CURLU *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_URL:\n    /*\n     * The URL to fetch.\n     */\n    if(data->state.url_alloc) {\n      /* the already set URL is allocated, free it first! */\n      Curl_safefree(data->state.url);\n      data->state.url_alloc = FALSE;\n    }\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->state.url = data->set.str[STRING_SET_URL];\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PORT:\n    /*\n     * The port number to use when getting the URL. 0 disables it.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.use_port = (unsigned short)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEOUT:\n    /*\n     * The maximum time you allow curl to use for a single transfer\n     * operation.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 0) && (arg <= (INT_MAX/1000)))\n      data->set.timeout = (unsigned int)arg * 1000;\n    else\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      uarg = UINT_MAX;\n    data->set.timeout = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT:\n    /*\n     * The maximum time you allow curl to use to connect.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 0) && (arg <= (INT_MAX/1000)))\n      data->set.connecttimeout = (unsigned int)arg * 1000;\n    else\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT_MS:\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      uarg = UINT_MAX;\n    data->set.connecttimeout = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_ACCEPTTIMEOUT_MS:\n    /*\n     * The maximum time for curl to wait for FTP server connect\n     */\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      uarg = UINT_MAX;\n    data->set.accepttimeout = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERPWD:\n    /*\n     * user:password to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = setstropt_userpwd(va_arg(param, char *),\n                               &data->set.str[STRING_USERNAME],\n                               &data->set.str[STRING_PASSWORD]);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_USERNAME:\n    /*\n     * authentication user name to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_USERNAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PASSWORD:\n    /*\n     * authentication password to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PASSWORD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_LOGIN_OPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * authentication options to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_OPTIONS],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_XOAUTH2_BEARER:\n    /*\n     * OAuth 2.0 bearer token to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_BEARER],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVE:\n    /*\n     * List of HOST:PORT:[addresses] strings to populate the DNS cache with\n     * Entries added this way will remain in the cache until explicitly\n     * removed or the handle is cleaned up.\n     *\n     * Prefix the HOST with plus sign (+) to have the entry expire just like\n     * automatically added entries.\n     *\n     * Prefix the HOST with dash (-) to _remove_ the entry from the cache.\n     *\n     * This API can remove any entry from the DNS cache, but only entries\n     * that aren't actually in use right now will be pruned immediately.\n     */\n    data->set.resolve = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    data->state.resolve = data->set.resolve;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROGRESSFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Progress callback function\n     */\n    data->set.fprogress = va_arg(param, curl_progress_callback);\n    if(data->set.fprogress)\n      data->progress.callback = TRUE; /* no longer internal */\n    else\n      data->progress.callback = FALSE; /* NULL enforces internal */\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_XFERINFOFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Transfer info callback function\n     */\n    data->set.fxferinfo = va_arg(param, curl_xferinfo_callback);\n    if(data->set.fxferinfo)\n      data->progress.callback = TRUE; /* no longer internal */\n    else\n      data->progress.callback = FALSE; /* NULL enforces internal */\n\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROGRESSDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the progress callback\n     */\n    data->set.progress_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYUSERPWD:\n    /*\n     * user:password needed to use the proxy\n     */\n    result = setstropt_userpwd(va_arg(param, char *),\n                               &data->set.str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME],\n                               &data->set.str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYUSERNAME:\n    /*\n     * authentication user name to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PROXYUSERNAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXYPASSWORD:\n    /*\n     * authentication password to use in the operation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_PROXYPASSWORD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOPROXY:\n    /*\n     * proxy exception list\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_NOPROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_RANGE:\n    /*\n     * What range of the file you want to transfer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SET_RANGE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RESUME_FROM:\n    /*\n     * Resume transfer at the given file position\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.set_resume_from = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_RESUME_FROM_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * Resume transfer at the given file position\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.set_resume_from = bigsize;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DEBUGFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * stderr write callback.\n     */\n    data->set.fdebug = va_arg(param, curl_debug_callback);\n    /*\n     * if the callback provided is NULL, it'll use the default callback\n     */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DEBUGDATA:\n    /*\n     * Set to a void * that should receive all error writes. This\n     * defaults to CURLOPT_STDERR for normal operations.\n     */\n    data->set.debugdata = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_STDERR:\n    /*\n     * Set to a FILE * that should receive all error writes. This\n     * defaults to stderr for normal operations.\n     */\n    data->set.err = va_arg(param, FILE *);\n    if(!data->set.err)\n      data->set.err = stderr;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADERFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set header write callback\n     */\n    data->set.fwrite_header = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_WRITEFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set data write callback\n     */\n    data->set.fwrite_func = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    if(!data->set.fwrite_func)\n      /* When set to NULL, reset to our internal default function */\n      data->set.fwrite_func = (curl_write_callback)fwrite;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_READFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Read data callback\n     */\n    data->set.fread_func_set = va_arg(param, curl_read_callback);\n    if(!data->set.fread_func_set) {\n      data->set.is_fread_set = 0;\n      /* When set to NULL, reset to our internal default function */\n      data->set.fread_func_set = (curl_read_callback)fread;\n    }\n    else\n      data->set.is_fread_set = 1;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SEEKFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Seek callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.seek_func = va_arg(param, curl_seek_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SEEKDATA:\n    /*\n     * Seek control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.seek_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * I/O control callback. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.ioctl_func = va_arg(param, curl_ioctl_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_IOCTLDATA:\n    /*\n     * I/O control data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.ioctl_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CERT],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CERT_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_TYPE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLCERTTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL certificate to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_CERT_TYPE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_KEY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file name of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEY_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds file content of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_KEY_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_TYPE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLKEYTYPE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds file type of the SSL key to use for proxy\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_TYPE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL or SSH private key password.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_KEYPASSWD:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL private key password for proxy.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_KEY_PASSWD_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE:\n    /*\n     * String that holds the SSL crypto engine.\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && argptr[0]) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE], argptr);\n      if(!result) {\n        result = Curl_ssl_set_engine(data, argptr);\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSLENGINE_DEFAULT:\n    /*\n     * flag to set engine as default.\n     */\n    Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ENGINE], NULL);\n    result = Curl_ssl_set_engine_default(data);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CRLF:\n    /*\n     * Kludgy option to enable CRLF conversions. Subject for removal.\n     */\n    data->set.crlf = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXYPROTOCOL:\n    /*\n     * Set to send the HAProxy Proxy Protocol header\n     */\n    data->set.haproxyprotocol = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP:\n    /*\n     * Set the client IP to send through HAProxy PROXY protocol\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    /* We enable implicitly the HAProxy protocol if we use this flag. */\n    data->set.haproxyprotocol = TRUE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_INTERFACE:\n    /*\n     * Set what interface or address/hostname to bind the socket to when\n     * performing an operation and thus what from-IP your connection will use.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DEVICE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_BINDLOCAL\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORT:\n    /*\n     * Set what local port to bind the socket to when performing an operation.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.localport = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_LOCALPORTRANGE:\n    /*\n     * Set number of local ports to try, starting with CURLOPT_LOCALPORT.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 65535))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.localportrange = curlx_sltous(arg);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_GSSAPI_DELEGATION:\n    /*\n     * GSS-API credential delegation bitmask\n     */\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    data->set.gssapi_delegation = (unsigned char)uarg&\n      (CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_POLICY_FLAG|CURLGSSAPI_DELEGATION_FLAG);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying.\n     */\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifypeer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifypeer =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifypeer;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    data->set.doh_verifypeer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYPEER:\n    /*\n     * Enable peer SSL verifying for proxy.\n     */\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifypeer =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->proxy_ssl_config.verifypeer =\n        data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifypeer;\n    }\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Obviously people are not reading documentation and too many thought\n       this argument took a boolean when it wasn't and misused it.\n       Treat 1 and 2 the same */\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifyhost =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifyhost;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate for DoH\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Treat both 1 and 2 as TRUE */\n    data->set.doh_verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3) ? TRUE : FALSE);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_VERIFYHOST:\n    /*\n     * Enable verification of the host name in the peer certificate for proxy\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    /* Treat both 1 and 2 as TRUE */\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifyhost = (bool)((arg & 3)?TRUE:FALSE);\n\n    /* Update the current connection proxy_ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->proxy_ssl_config.verifyhost =\n        data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.verifyhost;\n    }\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request()) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.ssl.primary.verifystatus = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n\n    /* Update the current connection ssl_config. */\n    if(data->conn) {\n      data->conn->ssl_config.verifystatus =\n        data->set.ssl.primary.verifystatus;\n    }\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_SSL_VERIFYSTATUS:\n    /*\n     * Enable certificate status verifying for DoH.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_cert_status_request()) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.doh_verifystatus = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * Set a SSL_CTX callback\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX))\n      data->set.ssl.fsslctx = va_arg(param, curl_ssl_ctx_callback);\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CTX_DATA:\n    /*\n     * Set a SSL_CTX callback parameter pointer\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX))\n      data->set.ssl.fsslctxp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_FALSESTART:\n    /*\n     * Enable TLS false start.\n     */\n    if(!Curl_ssl_false_start(data)) {\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    data->set.ssl.falsestart = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CERTINFO:\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CERTINFO))\n      data->set.ssl.certinfo = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify file name of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_PINNEDPUBLICKEY:\n    /*\n     * Set pinned public key for SSL connection.\n     * Specify file name of the public key in DER format.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY))\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_PINNEDPUBLICKEY_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info for SSL connection. Specify file name of the CA certificate\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB))\n      result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CAINFO],\n                               va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO:\n    /*\n     * Set CA info SSL connection for proxy. Specify file name of the\n     * CA certificate\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAFILE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAINFO_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds CA info for SSL connection proxy.\n     * Specify entire PEM of the CA certificate\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB))\n      result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_CAINFO_PROXY],\n                               va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    else\n#endif\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection. Specify directory name of the CA\n     * certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH))\n      /* This does not work on windows. */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CAPATH:\n    /*\n     * Set CA path info for SSL connection proxy. Specify directory name of the\n     * CA certificates which have been prepared using openssl c_rehash utility.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_CA_PATH))\n      /* This does not work on windows. */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CAPATH_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    else\n#endif\n      result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection. Specify file name of the CRL\n     * to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_CRLFILE:\n    /*\n     * Set CRL file info for SSL connection for proxy. Specify file name of the\n     * CRL to check certificates revocation\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CRLFILE_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer
