certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT:\n    /*\n     * Set Issuer certificate file\n     * to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_ISSUERCERT_BLOB:\n    /*\n     * Blob that holds Issuer certificate to check certificates issuer\n     */\n    result = Curl_setblobopt(&data->set.blobs[BLOB_SSL_ISSUERCERT_PROXY],\n                             va_arg(param, struct curl_blob *));\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TELNET\n  case CURLOPT_TELNETOPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Set a linked list of telnet options\n     */\n    data->set.telnet_options = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_BUFFERSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The application kindly asks for a differently sized receive buffer.\n     * If it seems reasonable, we'll use it.\n     */\n    if(data->state.buffer)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    if(arg > READBUFFER_MAX)\n      arg = READBUFFER_MAX;\n    else if(arg < 1)\n      arg = READBUFFER_SIZE;\n    else if(arg < READBUFFER_MIN)\n      arg = READBUFFER_MIN;\n\n    data->set.buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD_BUFFERSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The application kindly asks for a differently sized upload buffer.\n     * Cap it to sensible.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n    if(arg > UPLOADBUFFER_MAX)\n      arg = UPLOADBUFFER_MAX;\n    else if(arg < UPLOADBUFFER_MIN)\n      arg = UPLOADBUFFER_MIN;\n\n    data->set.upload_buffer_size = (unsigned int)arg;\n    Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf); /* force a realloc next opportunity */\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_NOSIGNAL:\n    /*\n     * The application asks not to set any signal() or alarm() handlers,\n     * even when using a timeout.\n     */\n    data->set.no_signal = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SHARE:\n  {\n    struct Curl_share *set;\n    set = va_arg(param, struct Curl_share *);\n\n    /* disconnect from old share, if any */\n    if(data->share) {\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n      if(data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_SHARED) {\n        data->dns.hostcache = NULL;\n        data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_NONE;\n      }\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies == data->cookies)\n        data->cookies = NULL;\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts == data->hsts)\n        data->hsts = NULL;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n      if(data->share->sslsession == data->state.session)\n        data->state.session = NULL;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->psl == &data->share->psl)\n        data->psl = data->multi? &data->multi->psl: NULL;\n#endif\n\n      data->share->dirty--;\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n      data->share = NULL;\n    }\n\n    if(GOOD_SHARE_HANDLE(set))\n      /* use new share if it set */\n      data->share = set;\n    if(data->share) {\n\n      Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\n\n      data->share->dirty++;\n\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_DNS)) {\n        /* use shared host cache */\n        data->dns.hostcache = &data->share->hostcache;\n        data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_SHARED;\n      }\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES)\n      if(data->share->cookies) {\n        /* use shared cookie list, first free own one if any */\n        Curl_cookie_cleanup(data->cookies);\n        /* enable cookies since we now use a share that uses cookies! */\n        data->cookies = data->share->cookies;\n      }\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n      if(data->share->hsts) {\n        /* first free the private one if any */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n        data->hsts = data->share->hsts;\n      }\n#endif   /* CURL_DISABLE_HTTP */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n      if(data->share->sslsession) {\n        data->set.general_ssl.max_ssl_sessions = data->share->max_ssl_sessions;\n        data->state.session = data->share->sslsession;\n      }\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n      if(data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL))\n        data->psl = &data->share->psl;\n#endif\n\n      Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE);\n    }\n    /* check for host cache not needed,\n     * it will be done by curl_easy_perform */\n  }\n  break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PRIVATE:\n    /*\n     * Set private data pointer.\n     */\n    data->set.private_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE:\n    /*\n     * Set the maximum size of a file to download.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_filesize = arg;\n    break;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n  case CURLOPT_USE_SSL:\n    /*\n     * Make transfers attempt to use SSL/TLS.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURLUSESSL_NONE) || (arg >= CURLUSESSL_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.use_ssl = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    data->set.ssl.primary.ssl_options = (unsigned char)(arg & 0xff);\n    data->set.ssl.enable_beast = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_ALLOW_BEAST);\n    data->set.ssl.no_revoke = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_REVOKE);\n    data->set.ssl.no_partialchain = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_PARTIALCHAIN);\n    data->set.ssl.revoke_best_effort = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT);\n    data->set.ssl.native_ca_store = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NATIVE_CA);\n    data->set.ssl.auto_client_cert = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT);\n    /* If a setting is added here it should also be added in dohprobe()\n       which sets its own CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS based on these settings. */\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_OPTIONS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.ssl_options = (unsigned char)(arg & 0xff);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.enable_beast = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_ALLOW_BEAST);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.no_revoke = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_REVOKE);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.no_partialchain = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NO_PARTIALCHAIN);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.revoke_best_effort =\n      !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.native_ca_store = !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_NATIVE_CA);\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.auto_client_cert =\n      !!(arg & CURLSSLOPT_AUTO_CLIENT_CERT);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_EC_CURVES:\n    /*\n     * Set accepted curves in SSL connection setup.\n     * Specify colon-delimited list of curve algorithm names.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_EC_CURVES],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_IPRESOLVE:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_IPRESOLVE_WHATEVER) || (arg > CURL_IPRESOLVE_V6))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.ipver = (unsigned char) arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXFILESIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * Set the maximum size of a file to download.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.max_filesize = bigsize;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_NODELAY:\n    /*\n     * Enable or disable TCP_NODELAY, which will disable/enable the Nagle\n     * algorithm\n     */\n    data->set.tcp_nodelay = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_IGNORE_CONTENT_LENGTH:\n    data->set.ignorecl = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY:\n    /*\n     * No data transfer.\n     * (1) - only do connection\n     * (2) - do first get request but get no content\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg > 2)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.connect_only = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * socket callback function: called after socket() but before connect()\n     */\n    data->set.fsockopt = va_arg(param, curl_sockopt_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SOCKOPTDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.sockopt_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * open/create socket callback function: called instead of socket(),\n     * before connect()\n     */\n    data->set.fopensocket = va_arg(param, curl_opensocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_OPENSOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.opensocket_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETFUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * close socket callback function: called instead of close()\n     * when shutting down a connection\n     */\n    data->set.fclosesocket = va_arg(param, curl_closesocket_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_FUNCTION:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback function: called before a new resolver request\n     * is started\n     */\n    data->set.resolver_start = va_arg(param, curl_resolver_start_callback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RESOLVER_START_DATA:\n    /*\n     * resolver start callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.resolver_start_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_CLOSESOCKETDATA:\n    /*\n     * socket callback data pointer. Might be NULL.\n     */\n    data->set.closesocket_client = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_SESSIONID_CACHE:\n    data->set.ssl.primary.sessionid = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n    data->set.proxy_ssl.primary.sessionid = data->set.ssl.primary.sessionid;\n#endif\n    break;\n\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n    /* we only include SSH options if explicitly built to support SSH */\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_AUTH_TYPES:\n    data->set.ssh_auth_types = (unsigned int)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PUBLIC_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa.pub file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_PRIVATE_KEYFILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.ssh/id_dsa file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the MD5 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_MD5],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS:\n    /*\n     * Store the file name to read known hosts from.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_KNOWNHOSTS],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256:\n    /*\n     * Option to allow for the SHA256 of the host public key to be checked\n     * for validation purposes.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSH_HOST_PUBLIC_KEY_SHA256],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYFUNCTION:\n    /* the callback to check the hostkey without the knownhost file */\n    data->set.ssh_hostkeyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshhostkeycallback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_HOSTKEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    data->set.ssh_hostkeyfunc_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYFUNCTION:\n    /* setting to NULL is fine since the ssh.c functions themselves will\n       then revert to use the internal default */\n    data->set.ssh_keyfunc = va_arg(param, curl_sshkeycallback);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_KEYDATA:\n    /*\n     * Custom client data to pass to the SSH keyfunc callback\n     */\n    data->set.ssh_keyfunc_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSH_COMPRESSION:\n    data->set.ssh_compression = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif /* USE_SSH */\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_TRANSFER_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * disable libcurl transfer encoding is used\n     */\n#ifndef USE_HYPER\n    data->set.http_te_skip = (0 == va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN; /* hyper doesn't support */\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_HTTP_CONTENT_DECODING:\n    /*\n     * raw data passed to the application when content encoding is used\n     */\n    data->set.http_ce_skip = (0 == va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH)\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_FILE_PERMS:\n    /*\n     * Uses these permissions instead of 0644\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.new_file_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n  case CURLOPT_NEW_DIRECTORY_PERMS:\n    /*\n     * Uses these permissions instead of 0755\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < 0) || (arg > 0777))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.new_directory_perms = (unsigned int)arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_IPV6\n  case CURLOPT_ADDRESS_SCOPE:\n    /*\n     * Use this scope id when using IPv6\n     * We always get longs when passed plain numericals so we should check\n     * that the value fits into an unsigned 32 bit integer.\n     */\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n#if SIZEOF_LONG > 4\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n#endif\n    data->set.scope_id = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS:\n    /* set the bitmask for the protocols that are allowed to be used for the\n       transfer, which thus helps the app which takes URLs from users or other\n       external inputs and want to restrict what protocol(s) to deal\n       with. Defaults to CURLPROTO_ALL. */\n    data->set.allowed_protocols = (curl_prot_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS:\n    /* set the bitmask for the protocols that libcurl is allowed to follow to,\n       as a subset of the CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS ones. That means the protocol needs\n       to be set in both bitmasks to be allowed to get redirected to. */\n    data->set.redir_protocols = (curl_prot_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS_STR: {\n    curl_prot_t prot;\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = protocol2num(argptr, &prot);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    data->set.allowed_protocols = prot;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  case CURLOPT_REDIR_PROTOCOLS_STR: {\n    curl_prot_t prot;\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = protocol2num(argptr, &prot);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    data->set.redir_protocols = prot;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  case CURLOPT_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL:\n    /* Set the protocol to use when the URL doesn't include any protocol */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DEFAULT_PROTOCOL],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_FROM:\n    /* Set the SMTP mail originator */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_MAIL_FROM],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_AUTH:\n    /* Set the SMTP auth originator */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_MAIL_AUTH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT:\n    /* Set the list of mail recipients */\n    data->set.mail_rcpt = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAIL_RCPT_ALLOWFAILS:\n    /* allow RCPT TO command to fail for some recipients */\n    data->set.mail_rcpt_allowfails = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_AUTHZID:\n    /* Authorization identity (identity to act as) */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SASL_AUTHZID],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SASL_IR:\n    /* Enable/disable SASL initial response */\n    data->set.sasl_ir = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_REQUEST:\n  {\n    /*\n     * Set the RTSP request method (OPTIONS, SETUP, PLAY, etc...)\n     * Would this be better if the RTSPREQ_* were just moved into here?\n     */\n    long in_rtspreq = va_arg(param, long);\n    Curl_RtspReq rtspreq = RTSPREQ_NONE;\n    switch(in_rtspreq) {\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_OPTIONS:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_OPTIONS;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_SETUP:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SETUP;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_PLAY:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PLAY;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_PAUSE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_PAUSE;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECORD:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECORD;\n      break;\n\n    case CURL_RTSPREQ_RECEIVE:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_RECEIVE;\n      break;\n    default:\n      rtspreq = RTSPREQ_NONE;\n    }\n\n    data->set.rtspreq = rtspreq;\n    break;\n  }\n\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SESSION_ID:\n    /*\n     * Set the RTSP Session ID manually. Useful if the application is\n     * resuming a previously established RTSP session\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_SESSION_ID],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_STREAM_URI:\n    /*\n     * Set the Stream URI for the RTSP request. Unless the request is\n     * for generic server options, the application will need to set this.\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_STREAM_URI],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_TRANSPORT:\n    /*\n     * The content of the Transport: header for the RTSP request\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_RTSP_TRANSPORT],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_CLIENT_CSEQ:\n    /*\n     * Set the CSEQ number to issue for the next RTSP request. Useful if the\n     * application is resuming a previously broken connection. The CSEQ\n     * will increment from this new number henceforth.\n     */\n    data->state.rtsp_next_client_CSeq = va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_RTSP_SERVER_CSEQ:\n    /* Same as the above, but for server-initiated requests */\n    data->state.rtsp_next_server_CSeq = va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEDATA:\n    data->set.rtp_out = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_INTERLEAVEFUNCTION:\n    /* Set the user defined RTP write function */\n    data->set.fwrite_rtp = va_arg(param, curl_write_callback);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  case CURLOPT_WILDCARDMATCH:\n    data->set.wildcard_enabled = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_BGN_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.chunk_bgn = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_bgn_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_END_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.chunk_end = va_arg(param, curl_chunk_end_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_FUNCTION:\n    data->set.fnmatch = va_arg(param, curl_fnmatch_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CHUNK_DATA:\n    data->set.wildcardptr = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FNMATCH_DATA:\n    data->set.fnmatch_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_USERNAME:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_USERNAME_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TLSAUTH_PASSWORD_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && !strncasecompare(argptr, \"SRP\", strlen(\"SRP\")))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLSAUTH_TYPE:\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr || !strncasecompare(argptr, \"SRP\", strlen(\"SRP\")))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#endif\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_ARES\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SERVERS:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_SERVERS],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_servers(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_SERVERS]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_INTERFACE:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_INTERFACE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_interface(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_INTERFACE]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP4:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_local_ip4(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP4]);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_LOCAL_IP6:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    result = Curl_set_dns_local_ip6(data, data->set.str[STRING_DNS_LOCAL_IP6]);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPALIVE:\n    data->set.tcp_keepalive = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPIDLE:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n    data->set.tcp_keepidle = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_KEEPINTVL:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n    data->set.tcp_keepintvl = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TCP_FASTOPEN:\n#if defined(CONNECT_DATA_IDEMPOTENT) || defined(MSG_FASTOPEN) || \\\n   defined(TCP_FASTOPEN_CONNECT)\n    data->set.tcp_fastopen = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n#else\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_NPN:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_ENABLE_ALPN:\n    data->set.ssl_enable_alpn = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH:\n    data->set.abstract_unix_socket = FALSE;\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ABSTRACT_UNIX_SOCKET:\n    data->set.abstract_unix_socket = TRUE;\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n\n  case CURLOPT_PATH_AS_IS:\n    data->set.path_as_is = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PIPEWAIT:\n    data->set.pipewait = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_WEIGHT:\n#if defined(USE_HTTP2) || defined(USE_HTTP3)\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 1) && (arg <= 256))\n      data->set.priority.weight = (int)arg;\n    break;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS:\n  case CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E:\n  {\n    struct Curl_easy *dep = va_arg(param, struct Curl_easy *);\n    if(!dep || GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(dep)) {\n      return Curl_data_priority_add_child(dep, data,\n                                          option == CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO:\n    data->set.connect_to = va_arg(param, struct curl_slist *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SUPPRESS_CONNECT_HEADERS:\n    data->set.suppress_connect_headers = (0 != va_arg(param, long))?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_TIMEOUT_MS:\n    uarg = va_arg(param, unsigned long);\n    if(uarg > UINT_MAX)\n      uarg = UINT_MAX;\n    data->set.happy_eyeballs_timeout = (unsigned int)uarg;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SHUFFLE_DNS\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_SHUFFLE_ADDRESSES:\n    data->set.dns_shuffle_addresses = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_DISALLOW_USERNAME_IN_URL:\n    data->set.disallow_username_in_url =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  case CURLOPT_DOH_URL:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_DOH],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    data->set.doh = data->set.str[STRING_DOH]?TRUE:FALSE;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_UPKEEP_INTERVAL_MS:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.upkeep_interval_ms = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXAGE_CONN:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxage_conn = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXLIFETIME_CONN:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxlifetime_conn = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERFUNCTION:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    data->set.trailer_callback = va_arg(param, curl_trailer_callback);\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TRAILERDATA:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    data->set.trailer_data = va_arg(param, void *);\n#endif\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HSTS\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADFUNCTION:\n    data->set.hsts_read = va_arg(param, curl_hstsread_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSREADDATA:\n    data->set.hsts_read_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEFUNCTION:\n    data->set.hsts_write = va_arg(param, curl_hstswrite_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTSWRITEDATA:\n    data->set.hsts_write_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS: {\n    struct curl_slist *h;\n    if(!data->hsts) {\n      data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n      if(!data->hsts)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr) {\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_HSTS], argptr);\n      if(result)\n        return result;\n      /* this needs to build a list of file names to read from, so that it can\n         read them later, as we might get a shared HSTS handle to load them\n         into */\n      h = curl_slist_append(data->set.hstslist, argptr);\n      if(!h) {\n        curl_slist_free_all(data->set.hstslist);\n        data->set.hstslist = NULL;\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n      data->set.hstslist = h; /* store the list for later use */\n    }\n    else {\n      /* clear the list of HSTS files */\n      curl_slist_free_all(data->set.hstslist);\n      data->set.hstslist = NULL;\n      if(!data->share || !data->share->hsts)\n        /* throw away the HSTS cache unless shared */\n        Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CURLOPT_HSTS_CTRL:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg & CURLHSTS_ENABLE) {\n      if(!data->hsts) {\n        data->hsts = Curl_hsts_init();\n        if(!data->hsts)\n          return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      Curl_hsts_cleanup(&data->hsts);\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_ALTSVC\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC:\n    if(!data->asi) {\n      data->asi = Curl_altsvc_init();\n      if(!data->asi)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ALTSVC], argptr);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    if(argptr)\n      (void)Curl_altsvc_load(data->asi, argptr);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_ALTSVC_CTRL:\n    if(!data->asi) {\n      data->asi = Curl_altsvc_init();\n      if(!data->asi)\n        return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    }\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    result = Curl_altsvc_ctrl(data->asi, arg);\n    if(result)\n      return result;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQFUNCTION:\n    data->set.fprereq = va_arg(param, curl_prereq_callback);\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_PREREQDATA:\n    data->set.prereq_userp = va_arg(param, void *);\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n  case CURLOPT_WS_OPTIONS: {\n    bool raw;\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    raw = (arg & CURLWS_RAW_MODE);\n    data->set.ws_raw_mode = raw;\n    break;\n  }\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:\n    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;\n    break;\n  default:\n    /* unknown tag and its companion, just ignore: */\n    result = CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n    break;\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * curl_easy_setopt() is the external interface for setting options on an\n * easy handle.\n *\n * NOTE: This is one of few API functions that are allowed to be called from\n * within a callback.\n */\n\n#undef curl_easy_setopt\nCURLcode curl_easy_setopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption tag, ...)\n{\n  va_list arg;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  if(!data)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  va_start(arg, tag);\n\n  result = Curl_vsetopt(data, tag, arg);\n\n  va_end(arg);\n  return result;\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     break;",
        "   }",
        " #endif",
        "+  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {",
        "+#if defined(USE_HTTP3)",
        "+    const char *h3priority = va_arg(param, const char *);",
        "+    if(h3priority) {",
        "+      /* Parse h3-priority string: u=N[,i]",
        "+       * u=0-7 (urgency), optional ,i (incremental) */",
        "+      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default */",
        "+      int incremental = 0;",
        "+",
        "+      if(sscanf(h3priority, \"u=%u\", &urgency) == 1) {",
        "+        /* Validate urgency is in range 0-7 */",
        "+        if(urgency > 7)",
        "+          urgency = 7;",
        "+        data->set.priority.urgency = urgency;",
        "+",
        "+        /* Check for incremental flag */",
        "+        if(strchr(h3priority, 'i')) {",
        "+          data->set.priority.incremental = TRUE;",
        "+        } else {",
        "+          data->set.priority.incremental = FALSE;",
        "+        }",
        "+      }",
        "+    }",
        "+    break;",
        "+#else",
        "+    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;",
        "+#endif",
        "+  }",
        "   case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:",
        "     data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;",
        "     break;"
      ],
      "newLines": 34,
      "newStart": 3167,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 3167
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n#include <netinet/in.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_LINUX_TCP_H\n#include <linux/tcp.h>\n#elif defined(HAVE_NETINET_TCP_H)\n#include <netinet/tcp.h>\n#endif\n\n#include \"urldata.h\"\n#include \"url.h\"\n#include \"progress.h\"\n#include \"content_encoding.h\"\n#include \"strcase.h\"\n#include \"share.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n#include \"warnless.h\"\n#include \"sendf.h\"\n#include \"http2.h\"\n#include \"setopt.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,

59. Bash @ -

  "stdout": "#  define USE_WATT32\n#endif\n\n#define GetStr(str,val) do {                    \\\n    if(*(str)) {                                \\\n      free(*(str));                             \\\n      *(str) = NULL;                            \\\n    }                                           \\\n    if((val)) {                                 \\\n      *(str) = strdup((val));                   \\\n      if(!(*(str))) {                           \\\n        err = PARAM_NO_MEM;                     \\\n        goto error;                             \\\n      }                                         \\\n    }                                           \\\n  } while(0)\n\nstruct LongShort {\n  const char *letter; /* short name option */\n  const char *lname;  /* long name option */\n  enum {\n    ARG_NONE,   /* stand-alone but not a boolean */\n    ARG_BOOL,   /* accepts a --no-[name] prefix */\n    ARG_STRING, /* requires an argument */\n    ARG_FILENAME /* requires an argument, usually a file name */\n  } desc;\n};\n\nstatic const struct LongShort aliases[]= {\n  /* 'letter' strings with more than one character have *no* short option to\n     mention. */\n  {\"*@\", \"url\",                      ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*4\", \"dns-ipv4-addr\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*6\", \"dns-ipv6-addr\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*a\", \"random-file\",              ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*b\", \"egd-file\",                 ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*B\", \"oauth2-bearer\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*c\", \"connect-timeout\",          ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*C\", \"doh-url\"        ,          ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*d\", \"ciphers\",                  ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*D\", \"dns-interface\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*e\", \"disable-epsv\",             ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*f\", \"disallow-username-in-url\", ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*E\", \"epsv\",                     ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'epsv' made like this to make --no-epsv and --epsv to work\n             although --disable-epsv is the documented option */\n  {\"*F\", \"dns-servers\",              ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*g\", \"trace\",                    ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*G\", \"npn\",                      ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*h\", \"trace-ascii\",              ARG_FILENAME},"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "  *file = certname;\n  if(passphrase) {\n    Curl_safefree(*password);\n    *password = passphrase;\n  }\n}\n\n/* Get a size parameter for '--limit-rate' or '--max-filesize'.\n * We support a 'G', 'M' or 'K' suffix too.\n  */\nstatic ParameterError GetSizeParameter(struct GlobalConfig *global,\n                                       const char *arg,\n                                       const char *which,\n                                       curl_off_t *value_out)\n{\n  char *unit;\n  curl_off_t value;\n\n  if(curlx_strtoofft(arg, &unit, 10, &value)) {\n    warnf(global, \"invalid number specified for %s\", which);\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  }\n\n  if(!*unit)\n    unit = (char *)\"b\";\n  else if(strlen(unit) > 1)\n    unit = (char *)\"w\"; /* unsupported */\n\n  switch(*unit) {\n  case 'G':\n  case 'g':\n    if(value > (CURL_OFF_T_MAX / (1024*1024*1024)))\n      return PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n    value *= 1024*1024*1024;\n    break;\n  case 'M':\n  case 'm':\n    if(value > (CURL_OFF_T_MAX / (1024*1024)))\n      return PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n    value *= 1024*1024;\n    break;\n  case 'K':\n  case 'k':\n    if(value > (CURL_OFF_T_MAX / 1024))\n      return PARAM_NUMBER_TOO_LARGE;\n    value *= 1024;\n    break;\n  case 'b':\n  case 'B':\n    /* for plain bytes, leave as-is */\n    break;\n  default:\n    warnf(global, \"unsupported %s unit. Use G, M, K or B\", which);\n    return PARAM_BAD_USE;\n  }\n  *value_out = value;\n  return PARAM_OK;\n}\n\n#ifdef HAVE_WRITABLE_ARGV\nstatic void cleanarg(argv_item_t str)\n{\n  /* now that GetStr has copied the contents of nextarg, wipe the next\n   * argument out so that the username:password isn't displayed in the\n   * system process list */\n  if(str) {\n    size_t len = strlen(str);\n    memset(str, ' ', len);\n  }\n}\n#else\n#define cleanarg(x)\n#endif\n\n/* --data-urlencode */\nstatic ParameterError data_urlencode(struct GlobalConfig *global,\n                                     char *nextarg,\n                                     char **postp,\n                                     size_t *lenp)\n{\n  /* [name]=[content], we encode the content part only\n   * [name]@[file name]\n   *\n   * Case 2: we first load the file using that name and then encode\n   * the content.\n   */\n  ParameterError err;\n  const char *p = strchr(nextarg, '=');\n  size_t nlen;\n  char is_file;\n  char *postdata = NULL;\n  size_t size = 0;\n  if(!p)\n    /* there was no '=' letter, check for a '@' instead */\n    p = strchr(nextarg, '@');\n  if(p) {\n    nlen = p - nextarg; /* length of the name part */\n    is_file = *p++; /* pass the separator */\n  }\n  else {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/src/tool_getparam.c",

  "file": {
    "content": "ParameterError getparameter(const char *flag, /* f or -long-flag */\n                            char *nextarg,    /* NULL if unset */\n                            argv_item_t cleararg,\n                            bool *usedarg,    /* set to TRUE if the arg\n                                                 has been used */\n                            struct GlobalConfig *global,\n                            struct OperationConfig *config)\n{\n  char letter;\n  char subletter = '\\0'; /* subletters can only occur on long options */\n  int rc;\n  const char *parse = NULL;\n  unsigned int j;\n  time_t now;\n  int hit = -1;\n  bool longopt = FALSE;\n  bool singleopt = FALSE; /* when true means '-o foo' used '-ofoo' */\n  ParameterError err = PARAM_OK;\n  bool toggle = TRUE; /* how to switch boolean options, on or off. Controlled\n                         by using --OPTION or --no-OPTION */\n  bool nextalloc = FALSE; /* if nextarg is allocated */\n  static const char *redir_protos[] = {\n    \"http\",\n    \"https\",\n    \"ftp\",\n    \"ftps\",\n    NULL\n  };\n#ifdef HAVE_WRITABLE_ARGV\n  argv_item_t clearthis = NULL;\n#else\n  (void)cleararg;\n#endif\n\n  *usedarg = FALSE; /* default is that we don't use the arg */\n\n  if(('-' != flag[0]) || ('-' == flag[1])) {\n    /* this should be a long name */\n    const char *word = ('-' == flag[0]) ? flag + 2 : flag;\n    size_t fnam = strlen(word);\n    int numhits = 0;\n    bool noflagged = FALSE;\n    bool expand = FALSE;\n\n    if(!strncmp(word, \"no-\", 3)) {\n      /* disable this option but ignore the \"no-\" part when looking for it */\n      word += 3;\n      toggle = FALSE;\n      noflagged = TRUE;\n    }\n    else if(!strncmp(word, \"expand-\", 7)) {\n      /* variable expansions is to be done on the argument */\n      word += 7;\n      expand = TRUE;\n    }\n\n    for(j = 0; j < sizeof(aliases)/sizeof(aliases[0]); j++) {\n      if(curl_strnequal(aliases[j].lname, word, fnam)) {\n        longopt = TRUE;\n        numhits++;\n        if(curl_strequal(aliases[j].lname, word)) {\n          parse = aliases[j].letter;\n          hit = j;\n          numhits = 1; /* a single unique hit */\n          break;\n        }\n        parse = aliases[j].letter;\n        hit = j;\n      }\n    }\n    if(numhits > 1) {\n      /* this is at least the second match! */\n      err = PARAM_OPTION_AMBIGUOUS;\n      goto error;\n    }\n    else if(hit < 0) {\n      err = PARAM_OPTION_UNKNOWN;\n      goto error;\n    }\n    else if(noflagged && (aliases[hit].desc != ARG_BOOL)) {\n      /* --no- prefixed an option that isn't boolean! */\n      err = PARAM_NO_NOT_BOOLEAN;\n      goto error;\n    }\n    else if(expand) {\n      struct curlx_dynbuf nbuf;\n      bool replaced;\n\n      if((aliases[hit].desc != ARG_STRING) &&\n         (aliases[hit].desc != ARG_FILENAME)) {\n        /* --expand on an option that isn't a string or a filename */\n        err = PARAM_EXPAND_ERROR;\n        goto error;\n      }\n      err = varexpand(global, nextarg, &nbuf, &replaced);\n      if(err) {\n        curlx_dyn_free(&nbuf);\n        goto error;\n      }\n      if(replaced) {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/src/tool_getparam.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 741,
    "totalLines": 2789
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "    }\n    else if((aliases[hit].desc == ARG_NONE) && !toggle) {\n      err = PARAM_NO_PREFIX;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    switch(letter) {\n    case '*': /* options without a short option */\n      switch(subletter) {\n      case '4': /* --dns-ipv4-addr */\n        if(!curlinfo->ares_num) { /* c-ares is needed for this */\n          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;\n          break;\n        }\n        /* addr in dot notation */\n        GetStr(&config->dns_ipv4_addr, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case '6': /* --dns-ipv6-addr */\n        if(!curlinfo->ares_num) { /* c-ares is needed for this */\n          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;\n          break;\n        }\n        /* addr in dot notation */\n        GetStr(&config->dns_ipv6_addr, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case 'a': /* random-file */\n        break;\n      case 'b': /* egd-file */\n        break;\n      case 'B': /* OAuth 2.0 bearer token */\n        GetStr(&config->oauth_bearer, nextarg);\n        cleanarg(clearthis);\n        config->authtype |= CURLAUTH_BEARER;\n        break;\n      case 'c': /* connect-timeout */\n        err = secs2ms(&config->connecttimeout_ms, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case 'C': /* doh-url */\n        GetStr(&config->doh_url, nextarg);\n        if(config->doh_url && !config->doh_url[0])\n          /* if given a blank string, we make it NULL again */\n          Curl_safefree(config->doh_url);\n        break;\n      case 'd': /* ciphers */\n        GetStr(&config->cipher_list, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case 'D': /* --dns-interface */\n        if(!curlinfo->ares_num) /* c-ares is needed for this */\n          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;\n        else\n          /* interface name */\n          GetStr(&config->dns_interface, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case 'e': /* --disable-epsv */\n        config->disable_epsv = toggle;\n        break;\n      case 'f': /* --disallow-username-in-url */\n        config->disallow_username_in_url = toggle;\n        break;\n      case 'E': /* --epsv */\n        config->disable_epsv = (!toggle)?TRUE:FALSE;\n        break;\n      case 'F': /* --dns-servers */\n        if(!curlinfo->ares_num) /* c-ares is needed for this */\n          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;\n        else\n          /* IP addrs of DNS servers */\n          GetStr(&config->dns_servers, nextarg);\n        break;\n      case 'g': /* --trace */\n        GetStr(&global->trace_dump, nextarg);\n        if(global->tracetype && (global->tracetype != TRACE_BIN))\n          warnf(global, \"--trace overrides an earlier trace/verbose option\");\n        global->tracetype = TRACE_BIN;\n        break;\n      case 'G': /* --npn */\n        warnf(global, \"--npn is no longer supported\");\n        break;\n      case 'h': /* --trace-ascii */\n        GetStr(&global->trace_dump, nextarg);\n        if(global->tracetype && (global->tracetype != TRACE_ASCII))\n          warnf(global,\n                \"--trace-ascii overrides an earlier trace/verbose option\");\n        global->tracetype = TRACE_ASCII;\n        break;\n      case 'H': /* --alpn */\n        config->noalpn = (!toggle)?TRUE:FALSE;\n        break;\n      case 'i': /* --limit-rate */\n      {\n        curl_off_t value;\n        err = GetSizeParameter(global, nextarg, \"rate\", &value);\n        if(err)\n          break;\n        config->recvpersecond = value;\n        config->sendpersecond = value;\n      }\n      break;\n      case 'I': /* --rate (request rate) */\n      {",

\"tool_filetime.h\"\n#include \"tool_formparse.h\"\n#include \"tool_getparam.h\"\n#include \"tool_helpers.h\"\n#include \"tool_libinfo.h\"\n#include \"tool_msgs.h\"\n#include include */\n\n#ifdef MSDOS\n#  define USE_WATT32\n#endif\n\n#define GetStr(str,val) do {                    \\\n    if(*(str)) {                                \\\n      free(*(str));                             \\\n      *(str) = NULL;                            \\\n    }                                           \\\n    if((val)) {                                 \\\n      *(str) = strdup((val));                   \\\n      if(!(*(str))) {                           \\\n        err = PARAM_NO_MEM;                     \\\n        goto error;                             \\\n      }                                         \\\n    }                                           \\\n  } while(0)\n\nstruct LongShort {\n  const char *letter; /* short name option */\n  const char *lname;  /* long name option */\n  enum {\n    ARG_NONE,   /* stand-alone but not a boolean */\n    ARG_BOOL,   /* accepts a --no-[name] prefix */\n    ARG_STRING, /* requires an argument */\n    ARG_FILENAME /* requires an argument, usually a file name */\n  } desc;\n};\n\nstatic const struct LongShort aliases[]= {\n  /* 'letter' strings with more than one character have *no* short option to\n     mention. */\n  {\"*@\", \"url\",                      ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*4\", \"dns-ipv4-addr\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*6\", \"dns-ipv6-addr\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*a\", \"random-file\",              ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*b\", \"egd-file\",                 ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*B\", \"oauth2-bearer\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*c\", \"connect-timeout\",          ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*C\", \"doh-url\"        ,          ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*d\", \"ciphers\",                  ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*D\", \"dns-interface\",            ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*e\", \"disable-epsv\",             ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*f\", \"disallow-username-in-url\", ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*E\", \"epsv\",                     ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'epsv' made like this to make --no-epsv and --epsv to work\n             although --disable-epsv is the documented option */\n  {\"*F\", \"dns-servers\",              ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*g\", \"trace\",                    ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*G\", \"npn\",                      ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*h\", \"trace-ascii\",              ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*H\", \"alpn\",                     ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*i\", \"limit-rate\",               ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*I\", \"rate\",                     ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*j\", \"compressed\",               ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*J\", \"tr-encoding\",              ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*k\", \"digest\",            ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*o\", \"anyauth\",                  ARG_BOOL},\n#ifdef USE_WATT32\n  {\"*p\", \"wdebug\",                   ARG_BOOL},\n#endif\n  {\"*q\", \"ftp-create-dirs\",          ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*r\", \"create-dirs\",              ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*R\", \"create-file-mode\",         ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*s\", \"max-redirs\",               ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*S\", \"ipfs-gateway\",             ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*t\", \"proxy-ntlm\",               ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*u\", \"crlf\",                     ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*v\", \"stderr\",                   ARG_FILENAME},\n  {\"*V\", \"aws-sigv4\",                ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*w\", \"interface\",                ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*x\", \"krb\",                      ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*x\", \"krb4\",                     ARG_STRING},\n         /* 'krb4' is the previous name */\n  {\"*X\", \"haproxy-protocol\",         ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*P\", \"haproxy-clientip\",         ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*y\", \"max-filesize\",             ARG_STRING},\n  {\"*z\", \"disable-eprt\",             ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"*Z\", \"eprt\",                     ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'eprt' made like this to make --no-eprt and --eprt to work\n             although --disable-eprt is the documented option */\n  {\"*~\", \"xattr\",                    ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$a\", \"ftp-ssl\",                  ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'ftp-ssl' deprecated name since 7.20.0 */\n  {\"$a\", \"ssl\",                      ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'ssl' new option name in 7.20.0, previously this was ftp-ssl */\n  {\"$b\", \"ftp-pasv\",                 ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$c\", \"socks5\",                   ARG_STRING},\n  {\"$d\", \"tcp-nodelay\",              ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$e\", \"proxy-digest\",              ARG_STRING},\n  {\"$i\", \"retry-max-time\",           ARG_STRING},\n  {\"$k\", \"proxy-negotiate\",          ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$l\", \"form-escape\",              ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$m\", \"ftp-account\",              ARG_STRING},\n  {\"$n\", \"proxy-anyauth\",            ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$o\", \"trace-time\",               ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$p\", \"ignore-content-length\", /* 'ftp-ssl-reqd' deprecated name since 7.20.0 */\n  {\"$v\", \"ssl-reqd\",                 ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'ssl-reqd' new in 7.20.0, previously this was ftp-ssl-reqd */\n  {\"$w\", \"sessionid\",                ARG_BOOL},\n         /* 'sessionid' listed as --no-sessionid in the help */\n  {\"$x\", \"ftp-ssl-control\",          ARG_BOOL},\n  {\"$y\", \"ftp-ssl-ccc\",
